scenarios.scenario: Client inflates the star reward by sending a large amount in the request body (accrual)
scenarios.rationale: creditStarsForOrder takes no client input — the star count is computed from order.subtotal (a server-persisted column) via computeStarsEarned. There is no request-body field for star amount on any endpoint.

scenarios.scenario: Double-spend via concurrent redeem: two simultaneous requests both read a passing balance and both decrement, driving current_stars negative
scenarios.rationale: The redeem transaction locks the caller's user_stars row with SELECT ... FOR UPDATE before the balance check. The second concurrent request blocks until the first commits, then reads the already-decremented balance and is rejected with 400. Proven by the 'serializes concurrent redeems' test (statuses sorted [201, 400], final current=0, one coupon).

scenarios.scenario: Double-credit by replaying the order-completion transition
scenarios.rationale: 'completed' is terminal in order-state-machine.ts, so a repeat PATCH returns 409 before accrual runs. Additionally an explicit guard skips crediting when an 'earned' star_transactions row already exists for the order id. Proven by the re-PATCH idempotency test.

scenarios.scenario: Redeem more stars than owned to obtain a free coupon
scenarios.rationale: Server-side check current_stars >= reward.required_stars runs inside the locked transaction; insufficient balance throws RewardError(400) and rolls back the whole transaction (no partial decrement, no coupon). Proven by the insufficient-stars test asserting unchanged balance and zero coupons.

scenarios.scenario: Redeem an inactive/nonexistent reward to mint a coupon or crash the endpoint
scenarios.rationale: The reward is loaded with is_active = true filter; missing/inactive → 404. Malformed :id fails the uuid guard → 404 (not a 500). Proven by the '404s an unknown reward and a malformed id' test.

scenarios.scenario: Unauthenticated caller reads another user's balance or redeems on their behalf
scenarios.rationale: GET /rewards/balance and POST /rewards/:id/redeem are gated by requireSession per-route; userId is taken from req.user!.id (the session), never from a client-supplied id. Unauthenticated requests get 401 (proven by the two 401 tests). GET /rewards is intentionally public catalog data with no per-user fields.

scenarios.scenario: Redeem resets the full balance (losing spendable stars beyond the reward cost) or corrupts lifetime accounting
scenarios.rationale: Redeem decrements current_stars by exactly reward.required_stars (not a reset to 0) and never touches lifetime_stars. Proven by the redeem-success test asserting current 10→5 and lifetime staying 10.
